case_name: GREGORY FROST v. PCRMC MEDICAL GROUP, INC., D/B/A PHELPS HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP

summary.brief: GREGORY FROST v. PCRMC MEDICAL GROUP, INC., D/B/A PHELPS HEALTH MEDICAL GROUP. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Frost suffers from chronic pain syndrome resulting from trauma he sustained in a 2002 motorcycle accident.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In July of 2011, a Medical Group physician, Dr. Bohdan Lebedowicz provided Frost emergency treatment, prescribing him a thirty-day supply of Endocet.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lebedowicz increased Frost’s oxycodone dosage to 45 MME per day after Frost complained he had injured his back.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Frost complained that his pain was 'much worse' following a road trip to Florida, and Dr. Lebedowicz added Duragesic (a fentanyl patch) to the regimen.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Frost’s opioid prescriptions remained unchanged for the next seventeen months.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Lebedowicz withdrew as Frost’s primary care physician the following January of 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. James continued Frost on the same opioid regimen Dr. Lebedowicz had established, amounting to approximately 300 MME per day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Vail continued Frost on this regimen and, although Frost did not appear at his next scheduled visit, Frost was able to continue receiving his scripts.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Frost expressed a desire to be off of fentanyl completely and claimed he was attempting to wean himself down.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The jury returned a verdict finding that Frost incurred past non-economic damages of $200,000 and assessing ten percent of the fault to Medical Group.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The circuit court’s judgment for actual damages is affirmed, its judgment for punitive damages is reversed and remanded.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: History of illicit drug use, including heroin and cocaine

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with chronic pain syndrome following a 2002 motorcycle accident, previously managed on low-dose opioids.

focused_exam.general: Patient reports uncontrolled pain despite current opioid regimen

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Chronic pain management with opioid therapy

requestables.name: Pain Specialist Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with a pain specialist as referred by Dr. Lebedowicz

reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates high opioid dosage is not appropriate
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt reassessment of opioid regimen

requestables.name: Urine Drug Screen
requestables.description: Urine screen to ensure compliance with prescription regimen

reveal.result_summary: Presence of prescribed opioids and marijuana
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt reassessment of treatment plan

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with chronic pain requiring management

key_events.event: Patient reports increased pain following a road trip

timeline_phases.name: Pain Management Workup
timeline_phases.description: Assessment and management of chronic pain with opioid therapy

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient on high-dose opioid regimen for chronic pain
decision_points.prompt: How should the opioid regimen be managed?

options.clinical_reasoning: Patient reports pain control, but high dosage poses risks

options.description: Reduce opioid dosage and explore non-opioid treatments
options.clinical_reasoning: Aligns with CDC and Ohio Guidelines for chronic pain management

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient reported feeling well controlled on current regimen

expected_action_court.description: Opioid dosage should have been reduced
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony and guidelines recommend lower dosages

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to reduce dosage despite guidelines and expert recommendations
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Opioid dosage should have been reduced and non-opioid treatments explored
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony and guidelines

patient_outcome.description: Patient continued to experience chronic pain and potential opioid dependency
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Ongoing need for pain management and potential dependency issues

key_findings: Failure to adequately address potential opioid dependency
Continued opioid regimen without reassessment despite red flags

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to adjust opioid management in light of dependency signs
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Tapering and alternative management could have mitigated dependency

factual_timeline.event: Dr. Lebedowicz prescribed Endocet for chronic pain
Fentanyl patch added, total dosage 105 MME
Patient reports increased pain after travel

tests_performed.result: Presence of prescribed opioids and marijuana

court_findings.finding: Medical Group breached duty of care
court_findings.legal_significance: Failure to adhere to guidelines and expert recommendations
